title: Simulated environment for presenting virtual objects and virtual resets
abstract: A modeling system stores a three-dimensional (3D) virtual object that corresponds to a real-world object, the 3D virtual object including a superimposition of an image area showing a portion of the real-world object on a face of a 3D shape. The modeling system stores a virtual reset that includes information about the 3D virtual object and a position of the 3D virtual object in the virtual reset. The modeling system presents the virtual reset, the presentation showing the 3D virtual object at the position.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A computer-implemented method in which one or more processing devices perform operations comprising: storing a three-dimensional (3D) virtual object that corresponds to a real- world object, the 3D virtual object including a superimposition of an image area showing a portion of the real- world object on a face of a 3D shape; storing a virtual reset that includes information about the 3D virtual object and a position of the 3D virtual object in the virtual reset; and presenting the virtual reset, the presentation showing the 3D virtual object at the position. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: generating the virtual object, wherein generating the virtual object comprises: receiving, via a user interface of a device, a selection of the 3D shape that includes a plurality of faces including the face; receiving an input indicating properties for the 3D shape; receiving an image showing a portion of a real- world object; determining an area of the image that corresponds to the portion of the real- world object; associating, in the virtual object, the area of the image with a face of the plurality of faces of the 3D shape; and associating, in the virtual object or in metadata of the virtual object, the properties with the virtual object. 3. The computer- implemented method of claim 2, wherein associating, in the virtual object, the area of the image with the face of the plurality of faces of the 3D shape comprises: presenting, via the user interface, the image imposed on the face, wherein boundaries of the portion of the real- world object in the image do not correspond with boundaries of the face; 

 providing, via the user interface, an editing interface object selectable to enable editing of the image; and editing the image, responsive to receiving inputs to the editing object, so that the boundaries of the portion of the real- world object in the image correspond to the boundaries of the face. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 2, wherein the properties comprise one or more of dimensions or a weight. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 2, further comprising: presenting, via the user interface, a set of three-dimensional shapes including the three-dimensional shape and one or more other three-dimensional shapes. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 5, wherein the set of three- dimensional shapes include one or more of a cube, a rectangular prism, a sphere, a spheroid, a cylinder, a cone, or a pyramid. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 , further comprising generating the 3D virtual reset, wherein generating the 3D virtual reset comprises: presenting, at a user interface, the 3D virtual object in the 3D virtual reset at a first position; receiving, via the user interface, an edit to the 3D virtual object in the 3D virtual reset; updating the presentation of the 3D virtual reset by showing the edit; and storing the 3D virtual reset by including, in the 3D virtual reset, information about the 3D virtual object and information about the edit. 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 7, wherein the information about the 3D virtual object comprises the 3D virtual object or a link to the 3D virtual object. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 7, wherein the edit comprises one or more of a change in position of the 3D virtual object in the 3D virtual reset from the first position to a second position or a rotation of the 3D virtual object. 10. The computer- implemented method of claim 7, wherein the edit comprises a change to the 3D virtual object comprising one or more of changes to images of one or more faces of the 3D virtual object or a resizing of the 3D virtual object. 11. The computer-implemented method of claim 7, further comprising: storing the edited 3D virtual object at a storage location in a data storage unit, wherein the information about the 3D virtual object and the information about the edit include the edited 3D virtual object or a link to the storage location of the edited 3D virtual object. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 7, further comprising: storing the edit at a storage location in a data storage unit, wherein the information about the 3D virtual object includes the 3D virtual object or a link to the 3D virtual object and the information about the edit includes the edit or a link to the storage location of the edit. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising: responsive to receiving an input in the augmented reality environment, moving the virtual reset within the augmented reality environment so that a location within the augmented reality environment of the virtual reset corresponds to a physical location in a physical environment of a physical reset to be assembled. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein presenting the virtual reset comprises presenting the virtual rest in a virtual reality environment. 15. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein presenting the virtual reset comprises presenting the virtual rest in an augmented reality environment. 16. A system comprising: a processor; and a non-transitory computer readable medium storing computer-readable program instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the system to: 

 storing a three-dimensional (3D) virtual object that corresponds to a real- world object, the 3D virtual object including a superimposition of an image area showing a portion of the real- world object on a face of a 3D shape; storing a virtual reset that includes information about the 3D virtual object and a position of the 3D virtual object in the virtual reset; and presenting the virtual reset, the presentation showing the 3D virtual object at the position. 17. The system of claim 16, the operations further comprising: generating the virtual object, wherein generating the virtual object comprises: receiving, via a user interface of a device, a selection of the 3D shape that includes a plurality of faces including the face; receiving an input indicating properties for the 3D shape; receiving an image showing a portion of a real- world object; determining an area of the image that corresponds to the portion of the real- world object; associating, in the virtual object, the area of the image with a face of the plurality of faces of the 3D shape; and associating, in the virtual object or in metadata of the virtual object, the properties with the virtual object. 18 The system of claim 16, the operations further comprising generating the 3D virtual reset, wherein generating the 3D virtual reset comprises: presenting, at a user interface, the 3D virtual object in the 3D virtual reset at a first position; receiving, via the user interface, an edit to the 3D virtual object in the 3D virtual reset; updating the presentation of the 3D virtual reset by showing the edit; and storing the 3D virtual reset by including, in the 3D virtual reset, information about the 3D virtual object and information about the edit. 19. The system of claim 16 the operations further comprising: 

 responsive to receiving an input in the augmented reality environment, moving the virtual reset within the augmented reality environment so that a location within the augmented reality environment of the virtual reset corresponds to a physical location in a physical environment of a physical reset to be assembled. 20. A non-transitory computer-readable medium having program code that is stored thereon, the program code executable by one or more processing devices for performing operations comprising: storing a three-dimensional (3D) virtual object that corresponds to a real- world object, the 3D virtual object including a superimposition of an image area showing a portion of the real- world object on a face of a 3D shape; storing a virtual reset that includes information about the 3D virtual object and a position of the 3D virtual object in the virtual reset; and presenting the virtual reset, the presentation showing the 3D virtual object at the position.
